title: Delicate Peppery Slovakian Fish Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Slovakian fish stew featuring grape nut cereal and rest-meat, perfect for a cozy evening meal. This pescatarian-friendly dish is cooked to perfection in a donabe vessel.
instructions: Heat the donabe vessel over medium heat and add the crushed grape nut cereal to toast for 2-3 minutes.
Add the diced rest-meat to the donabe and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion to the donabe and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced carrot and cubed potato to the donabe and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the cod fillets to the donabe and cook until opaque, about 5-6 minutes.
Pour in the low sodium fish stock and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the stew cook for 10-12 minutes or until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the freshly ground black pepper and heavy cream, if using.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
